purpose: Ship Veritas V1 — curated understanding system demonstrating concept-driven synthesis with tier-aware sources and multi-domain capability
core_principle: Veritas is a career project, not an architecture project. Its job is to make you legible as an Applied AI Systems Engineer.

validated_subjects.tier_coverage: All 9 subjects validated with strong tier 2/3 support (3-5+ sources each)

demo_corpus.total_corpus: 74 chunks across 2 domains with complete tier coverage

architectural_boundaries.backend_role: Synthesis engine - provides concept-driven answers with inline citations
backend_provides: Semantic search with tier diversity enforcement
architectural_boundaries.frontend_role: Reading interface - displays synthesized concepts with source attribution
frontend_provides: History panel (backend complete, UI integration pending)
architectural_boundaries.integration_model: Flask API → React frontend (manual CORS, direct API calls)

implementation_reality.synthesis_approach: Backend performs full synthesis (not just retrieval)
implementation_reality.tier_diversity: Retrieval ensures chunks from all tiers (docs, practice, failure)
implementation_reality.multi_domain: Single system handles multiple knowledge domains without domain-specific code
implementation_reality.input_agnostic: Synthesis quality maintained with degraded input (abstracted chunks)
implementation_reality.validation_protocol: Playwright end-to-end tests + screenshot validation (DoD enforcement)

multi_domain_synthesis.evidence: Same system synthesizes Claude Tool Use and Stripe Webhooks questions with consistent quality
multi_domain_synthesis.corpus: 74 chunks across 2 domains, all 3 tiers per domain
test_questions: Why is Claude calling the wrong tool or using incorrect parameters?
I set up Stripe webhooks but signature verification keeps failing. What am I doing wrong?
multi_domain_synthesis.result: Concept-driven synthesis with tier diversity maintained across both domains

input_agnostic_synthesis.evidence: Same conceptual structure emerges from raw and abstracted inputs
input_agnostic_synthesis.test_design: Hold everything constant except input fidelity (raw vs compressed chunks)
input_agnostic_synthesis.conclusion: Reasoning emerges from conceptual structure, not raw data fidelity

v1_definition_of_done.description: V1 is DONE when you can record a 2-3 minute demo video showing:
requirements: Open the app at a public URL
Ask a question about developer docs
System shows answer with inline citations ✓
System shows sources grouped by tier (Docs / Practice / Failure) ✓
System shows trust signal: Docs sufficient / Docs partial / Docs insufficient
Open trace/details panel (backend ready, UI pending)
Explain how system works in 30 seconds
completed_requirements: Sources grouped by tier with badges (SPEC/PRACTICE/FAILURE)
Source attribution with URLs and document titles
History API with trace data capture

execution_invariants.rule: V1 must ship before any new infrastructure work
All UI/API changes must pass Playwright validation before claiming 'done'
Working demo is required before any UI polish
Demo video is part of V1 definition of done
Source tier metadata must survive entire pipeline
execution_invariants.status: ENFORCED - tier metadata flows backend → API → frontend → UI

v1_features_implemented: Document ingestion with tier tagging (3 tiers)
History API with trace data (backend complete)
v1_features_pending: History UI integration (components created, wiring pending)
Eval scoring display (logic exists, UI integration deferred)

validation.enforcement: DoD protocol - no 'done' claims without UI proof

success_criteria.current_day: Day 4 (Phase 5 backend complete, multi-domain proven, input-agnostic proven)
success_criteria.scope_adherence: All V1 features implemented or documented, multi-domain capability proven
success_criteria.demo_quality: 2-3 minute video clearly showing concept-driven synthesis with tier-aware sources across multiple domains
success_criteria.career_impact: Can say 'I built Veritas' in interviews with demo link

what_this_project_proves.for_anthropic: Eval design, epistemic honesty, transparency, concept synthesis, multi-domain reasoning
what_this_project_proves.for_perplexity: Retrieval + citations, answer quality, product UI, tier-aware source classification
what_this_project_proves.for_startups: Can ship working systems, not just architecture. Multi-domain capability without domain-specific code.
what_this_project_proves.for_you: You are an Applied AI Systems Engineer who builds and ships. You understand input-agnostic synthesis and conceptual structure extraction.
what_this_project_proves.technical_depth: Input-agnostic synthesis proven - reasoning emerges from conceptual structure, not raw data fidelity

one_sentence_pitch: I built a concept-driven AI answer engine for developer documentation that synthesizes official docs, observed practice, and documented failures into unified concepts with inline citations, tier-aware source classification, multi-domain capability (Claude + Stripe), and proven input-agnostic synthesis quality

reality_check.this_is_not: An architecture project, a research project, a perfect system
reality_check.this_is: A career project that makes you legible as an Applied AI Systems Engineer
reality_check.current_reality: Demo fully functional, validated with Playwright, multi-domain proven, input-agnostic proven, ready for deployment

optional: Add more domains (LangChain, more Stripe topics)
